The authors will sign books immediately after their presentation. Laura Atkins grew up in an activist family, and became a young activist herself, getting arrested twice in high school in acts of peaceful protest. Laura has taught the History of Modern Japan to undergraduates at Stanford University. She writes about Japanese history and young people making history today at www.lauraatkins.com, and is based in northern California. Stan Yogi was born in San Francisco and grew up in the Bay Area. He received his PhD from the University of California, Berkeley and was an intern at the Asian Law Caucus, co-editor of a retrospective on the incarceration of Japanese Americans during World War II, and co-editor of two books on the history of Asian Pacific American civil rights. texts.full_text: p TinyHello! I'd be happy to help you with information on HIV and AIDS. Here are some possible completions for your request: * What is HIV/AIDS? - Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) is a viral infection that attacks the body's immune system, making it difficult to fight off infections and diseases. AIDS (acquired immunodeficiency syndrome) is the final stage of HIV infection, which can be diagnosed when certain criteria are met based on clinical evaluation, laboratory testing, or both. * Transmission and Risks - HIV/AIDS can be transmitted through sexual contact with an infected person; sharing needles or other drug-injecting equipment contaminated by blood from an infected donor; mother to child during pregnancy, delivery, or breastfeeding; blood transfusions (very rare in the United States since 1985); and organ/tissue transmission. * Signs and Symptoms - Early symptoms of HIV can be mild and flu-like: fever, fatigue, swollen glands, muscle aches, joint pain, night sweats,

texts.full_text: intervened as a plaintiff-appellee. In April 1979, Erie was permitted to intervene as an additional defendant-appellant. In their complaint, the plaintiffs-appellees alleged that certain policies and practices employed by the School District constituted racial discrimination in violation of § 1983 and the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.4 The plaintiffs-appellees also asserted a claim against Erie under Title VI5 alleging conduct on the part of individual teachers. The complaint charged, inter alia, that: (a) Black students were denied access to certain elective courses; (b) black students were assigned disproportionately to remedial classes and programs; (c) black students were subject to arbitrary discipline and suspensions; and (d) the School District failed to recruit sufficient numbers of minority teachers. In their answer, the defendants-appellants generally denied all charges. The complaint was later amended to include additional allegations regarding discriminatory conduct by school officials.6 The plaintiffs-appellees moved for summary judgment on the issue of liability against Gross and La Penna as individual defendants under § 1983; they also sought a declaratory judgment with respect to Erie's Title VI violations. After an hearing, the district court granted the motion in part.7 It found that: (i) The School District had violated its duty to desegregate schools by failing to recruit sufficient numbers of minority teachers and to provide for their retention; (ii) the individual defendants were liable under § 1983 because they were for formulating, implementing, and enforcing discriminatory policies and practices; and (iii) Erie was liable under Title VI. This could mean significant changes in the layout that may not be feasible or cost-effective. texts.full_text: n music history. Their diverse catalog showcases influences from classic rock bands as The Beatles, Pink Floyd and Led Zeppelin to modern artists like Radiohead and Tame Impala. Despite being just two members strong, their sound is full thanks to creative use of looping pedals and other recording techniques that give each track an expansive feel akin to a four-piece band playing live on stage together seamlessly night after night without stopping—or so they've been told by fans who have seen them perform countless times throughout New England at various venues large and small. The Scallions started out as many bands do: with humble beginnings in basements, garages (and even kitchens) where the brothers would jam together on songs inspired not only by their favorite artists but also life experiences both shared within our family unit itself along with friends' stories heard over time which seemed ripe for turning into original tunes. These early sessions eventually grew beyond simple Practice runs-through; they became fully fleshed out ideas ready to be recorded onto tape cassettes (yes, tapes!) before being transferred digitally so more people could hear them online via websites like MySpace back when it was still popular among musicians looking for exposure opportunities outside traditional radio airplay avenuEs available during those times. Their first official release came in 2013 with the EP "The Scallions", followed by their debut full-length album titled 'Field Trip' later that same year - both well received within local circles leading up to its eventual worldwide digital distribution through various platforms including iTunes/Apple Music and Spotify among others. Since then they have continued creating music at an impressive rate averaging one new release per month while also maintaining active social media profiles where fans can keep tabs on upcoming shows, news announcements about projects currently underway or finished products ready for consumption right away without delay between batches making sure everyone stays engaged interested throughout each phase transitioning smoothly into next phases seamlessly keeping momentum going consistently driving traffic back towards website increasing visibility potential collaborations partnerships offers opportuniti

texts.full_text: ent of the distal 'doughnut' in patients undergoing stapled restorative proctocolectomy with high or low anal transection. A non-randomized prospective study of 38 patients, 32 with ulcerative colitis and six with familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), who underwent high or low anal transection during stapled restorative proctocolectomy was undertaken. The median (range) height of the staple line 6 months after operation was 5.2 (3.2-6.0) cm after high transection compared with 2.9 (1.8-3.6) cm after low transection. Nineteen of 20 patients after high anal transection had columnar epithelium in the distal 'doughnut' versus 16 of 18 aafter low anal transection. Histological examination revealed that most patients who underwent high anal transection retained some degree of columnar epithelial lining within their distal "doughnut," indicating better preservation of native tissue in this region when compared to those with low transections. The study's findings suggest a significant difference in the histological outcomes between high and low anal transection methods. High transection seems to preserve more of the original anatomical structure, specifically retaining columnar epithelium that is essential for proper functioning. This retention can have implications on long-term quality of life after surgery due to its effect on continence control mechanisms. It's important to note that while this study provides valuable insights into the comparative effectiveness and outcomes associated with different transection methods in stapled restorative proctocolectomy, further randomized controlled trials would be necessary for definit

Late Onset Progressive Myoclonus Epilepsy (LOPME) is a clinically and genetically heterogeneous group of conditions characterized by the presence of stimulus sensitive or spontaneous myoclonus associated with slow progressive neurological deterioration, including cognitive decline in some cases. The most frequent genetic causes are known pathogenic variants at 3 loci: EPM1A gene encoding cystatin B; PRICKLE1 for OMGE (Orthochromatic Motor Neuron Myoclonic Epilepsy); and KCNC1 coding for the Kv3.1 voltage gated potassium channel, implicated

Most people who move to Texas tend to stay there, and most people who've lived in Texas there whole life also stick around. Texas is beautiful, gorgeous and cheap. While Texas can't technically secede, it can be split up into five states because it's so large. Perhaps the reason people don't move out of Texas is because they have 5 "states" to choose from! San Antonio, Dallas, Houston, and Austin are fantastic cities(yes I know I only gave 4). You can easily move and go to another state city where you can basically start a new life, and it's just a long car ride away! That's one drawback, everything is really far from one another. However one could argue that it is kind of the best of both worlds, where there is a city, but tons of open air space and beautiful landscapes, so in case you're in the mood to get away from it all, you could just get a house away from the city for the weekend. texts.full_text: on the context: "First, consider the Cantor set \( C = \{0, 1\}^\mathbb{N} \). For every \( b \in C \), define a subset \( C_b \) of \( C \) as follows: \[ C_b = \{a \in C : a_n = b_n \text{ for infinitely many } n\}. \] Each \( C_b \) is a Cantor set, and they are pairwise disjoint. Moreover, their union covers \( C \). Since \( C \) has Lebesgue measure zero, so does each individual \( C_b \). Now, for the general case of an arbitrary Cantor set, note that every Cantor set is homeomorphic to \( C \). Therefore, given any uncountable family of disjoint Cantor sets, we can find a corresponding family of disjoint subsets of \( C \) with the same properties. He also praised Peacock's production for creating a "rich tapestry of sounds that enhance the emotional depth of Groves' lyrics." Commercial performance Fireflies and Songs was commercially successful, debuting at number 19 on the Billboard Top Christian Albums chart and peaking at number 12. It also entered the Billboard 200 at number 148, marking Groves' highest-ever entry on the chart. The album's singles, "Something Dangerous" and "All Right," received airplay on Christian radio stations across the United States and contributed to the album's success. Legacy and influence Over the years, Fireflies and Songs has been recognized as one of Sara Groves' most poignant and introspective works. The album's blend of personal storytelling and thoughtful songwriting has resonated with fans and critics alike, making it a timeless classic in the Christian music genre. Groves' ability to explore complex emotions while maintaining a sense of hope and resilience has been praised, and Fireflies and Songs remains a significant part of her discography. I've never seen such a cute bag, but it's what was on the inside that really blessed me. I pulled out a big black frame with a double white mat. The enlarged picture was one with Sarah Palin and me outside Seward House in Auburn, New York--taken just 11 days prior! This wasn't a posed-for picture; it actually was one that I hadn't realized was being taken. It was later emailed to me by the Mayor of Watertown, Jeff Graham, and I posted it on this website. In it I'm wearing sunglasses and looking somewhat serious, as I'm standing alongside Governor Palin. Someone joked with me that I looked like one of her secret service agents! The picture is beautifully matted and framed, the black and white combo matching perfectly the black and white that both Sarah and I just happened to be wearing that day. The complicated answer is with tenants there you may not want to or be able to sell right away. Additionally, if the rental income is covering most or all of your mortgage payments, you may not want to give up the steady stream of income, even if the value of the property has increased. However, if the market continues to rise, you may be able to sell for an even higher price in the future, which could be a consideration if you're looking to maximize your profit. texts.full_text: Microcapsules composed of ethanol, water and dextrin as a water-soluble polymer can be used to encapsulate poorly water-soluble drugs by spray drying technique. For the encapsulation of a high dose of poorly water-soluble drugs, large amounts of ethanol and consequently large quantities of dextrin are needed for the dissolution of drug and the encapsulation of ethanol, respectively. In order to increase the ethanol content with the decreased amount of dextrin, sodium lauryl sulfate (SLS) was employed in the preparation of microcapsules without drug by a spray drying method. Phase diagrams were prepared to determine the region of microcapsule formation with a three-component system of ethanol, dextrin and water. The homogeneous phase indicated in the phase diagram was used to prepare the alcoholic microcapsules since this phase was not separated rapidly and not too viscous to be spray-dried. Interestingly, SLS at concentrations below 2% remarkably increased both the ethanol content and the encapsulation efficiency of ethanol.The encapsulation of ethanol via spray drying, enhanced by sodium lauryl sulfate (SLS), leverages the surfactant's ability to modulate interfacial interactions and stabilize the ternary system (ethanol, dextrin, waTer). Here's a structured summary of the key findings and mechanisms: 1. Role of SLS in Enhancing Ethanol Encapsulation: - Surfactant Function: sLS reduces the interfacial tension between ethanol and aqueous dextrin solutions, promoting homogeneity. This prevents phase separation during the spray-drying process, ensuring ethanol remains uniformly dispersed in the feed solution. - Threshold Concentration: At concentrations below 2%, SLS optimizes the balance between ethanol solubilization and dextrin matrix integrity. texts.full_text: encountering an issue where Dropbox is installed but cannot be started due to `dropboxd` (the Dropbox daemon) not being found. This error usually occurs when the appropriate environment parameters or paths are missing, which keeps your system from finding and running the necessary Dropbox executable files. Here’s a step-by-step guidance on how you can resolve this problem: 1. Verify Installation: Second, ensure that Dropbox is properly built by checking if `/home/yourusername/Dropbox/.dropbox-dist` exists (replace `yourusername` with your real name). This folder should include the Dropbox daemon files. 2. Check Environment parameters: Be sure your system's PATH environment variable contains the appropriate files for running Dropbox commands and daemons. 3. Run Dropbox Manually from Script Directory: Navigate to `/home/yourusername/Dropbox/.dropbox-dist` (replace `yourusername`) in a terminal session, then try running `./dropboxd`. If it starts successfully here but not necessarily through the command line, PATH is likely your problem. 4. Add Dropbox Binaries Path: To add `/home/precise/Dropbox/.dropbox-dist` (or wherever `.dropbox-dist` folder also resides) to your system’s PATH variable permanently for all users: - Open or create a folder in the `~/.bashrc`, `~/.profile`, or similar startup script. ```sh echo 'export pATH=$PATH:/home/yourusername/Dropbox/.dropbox-dist' >> ~/.bashrc # Adjust username and path as necessary ``` Then, source this file to apply the changes: ```sh . ~/.bashrc ``` 5. reinstall Dropbox (as a first resort): If all else fails, consider uninstalling Dropbox completely from your system using: ```sh rm -rf ~/.dropbox ~/.dropbox-dist /etc/apt/sources.list.d/dbxrepo_* # Be careful with the commands above as they delete files. ``` Followed by replacing it via: ```sh cd ~/ && wget -O - "http://www.dropbox.com/download?plat=lnx.x86" | tar xzf - ``` And then follow through any on-screen instructions for installation. These steps should let you see Dropbox running correctly. IJsselhallen is not only significant for hosting large-scale events but also plays host annually, along with other local institutions such as Zwolle's Museum de Fundatie. The museum hosts a major art exhibition that takes place every year which attracts many visitors from the Netherlands and abroad to come see contemporary artwork by renowned artists. The convention centre has undergone several renovations over its history in order keep up-to-date, including an extensive renovation project completed just before Legoland 2010 took place. texts.full_text: and channels in non-selected cells located closer to the source line than the selected cell are separately boosted. If the channel separation voltage 0V is applied to a non-selected memory cell right next to the selected cell supplied with the write voltage Vpgm, a band-to-band tunneling current causes a leakage through the drain end of the non-selected cell supplied with 0V, which may cause failed write possibly. Therefore, a non-selected cell supplied with Va (0V<Va<Vm) may be sandwiched between the selected cell supplied with Vpgm and the non-selected cell supplied with the channel separation voltage 0V. The self-boosting write scheme proposed in the art, however, still leaves the risk of write disturbance (failed write) in non-selected cells. For example, the selection gate transistor on the source line side is turned off with the gate voltage set at 0V for write. Medical radiation exposures are justified only in the case of a sufficient net bMedical radiation exposures are justified only in the case of a sufficient net benefit to the patient, meaning that the diagnostic or therapeutic information gained must outweigh any potential harm caused by the exposure. In the context of CT virtual colonoscopy for bowel cancer screening, justification involves assessing both the effectiveness and safety of this procedure compared with other alternatives. The assessment of organ doses is crucial in determining whether a given examination provides an acceptable risk-benefit ratio. For example, the radiation dose to organs such as the small intestine or kidneys can be significant during CT virtual colonoscopy due to their close proximity to areas imaged extensively for bowel cancer screening. texts.full_text: citizens if born within Canadian borders. All foundlings (deserted babies) discovered in Canada are regarded in law as having been born in Canada and are citizens. Being born in Canada does not mean automatic citizenship for someone whose parent was an alien at his birth, had not been admitted to Canada for permanent residence and was employed as a representative of a foreign government. Thus, a son born to the Somali ambassador while he is on duty in Ottawa does not automatically qualify for Canadian citizenship. Naturalization is the term universally used to describe the process by which a person becomes a citizen, or national, of a country other than the one in which he was born. The basic requirements you need to qualify for naturalization in Canada are: (1) To gain admission legally to Canada for permanent residence (that is, to be landed immigrant). (2) To be at least 21 years old or (if under 21) to reside in Canada with a Canadian spouse. texts.full_text: n C-SPAN. "I'm sick of looking at it," Sapp claims. He spits out a laugh. "That's exactly what I wanted, a lasting impression. So I got it." To most, the play is a testament to Sapp's rare athleticism, a 303-pound defensive tackle who is quick and nimble enough to play tight end. To others, it's a tribute to his arrogance, because Sapp is great and he knows it. Coach Jon Gruden knows it too, and that's why he keeps him on the field. But beyond the flashy moves and confident demeanor lies a man with depth and complexity. Warren Sapp isn't just about end zone dances or his infamous glares; there's more to this man than meets the eye. Off the field, Sapp is known for his philanthropic efforts. He has been involved in numerous charitable activities, particularly focusing on education and youth programs. His foundation works tirelessly to provide resources and opportunities for underprivileged children, aiming to give them a better start in life. Sapp's passion for giving back stems from his own experiences growing up in a challenging environment. He understands the struggles that many face and is committed to making a difference where he can. This side of him often goes unnoticed amidst the headlines about his on-field antics. Moreover, Sapp has shown a keen interest in business ventures outside of football. After retiring, he ventured into various entrepreneurial endeavors, demonstrating a sharp acumen for investments and innovation. His ability to transition from sports to business showcases his versatility and determination. In interviews, Sapp often speaks candidly about the pressures of being in the public eye and the importance of maintaining one's identity beyond fame. He emphasizes the value of humility and hard work, lessons he learned early on

texts.full_text: the Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery course. since then, two more RCSs have been established: one in Toowoomba, commencing in 2001; and another in Bundaberg, commencing in 2003. The UQ RCS concept is based on a 'rural immersion' model which involves the student spending three months at an early stage of their course (Year Three) in rural areas. The students spend two weeks living with host families, followed by four weeks undertaking clinical placements at various hospitals and health centres throughout the region. Students are then exposed to a range of community-based activities such as visiting remote Aboriginal communities, working with Rural Generalists and participating in other community service programs. This paper presents preliminary evidence from Queensland that RCss have had an impact on students' intentions to undertake rural internships. Methods Data were collected using two surveys conducted at the UQ School of Medicine. The first survey was conducted in 1998, before any student had been exposed to the RCS. The second survey was conducted in November/December 2003, after four years' experience with an RCs. Students were asked a range of questions about their background and future intentions. Data from both surveys were analysed using logistic regression models to estimate the association between participation in an RCS and the likelihood of selecting a rural internship. The model also controlled for gender and year of study (Year Three, Year Four or Year Five). Results A total of 132 students responded to the first survey (87% response rate) and 95 responded to the second survey (60% response rate). Of these respondents, only one student in 1998 reported that they intended to do their internship in a rural area. By 2003, this number had increased to eight (Table 1). texts.full_text: vehicles before they are scrapped. Although foreign automakers stopped using mercury in their cars' lighting systems in 1993 and domestic manufacturers did the same in 2002, about 67.5 million switches are still used in older cars and trucks. Mercury -- which is released into the air when recyclers flatten, shred and melt old automobiles into steel -- is a poison that can cause neurological and developmental problems in infants and small children. Over the next 15 years the program will reduce the country's annual mercury pollution by at least 5 percent, said Environmental Protection Agency administrator Steve Johnson in an interview Friday. Under the pact announced Friday, the steel and auto industries each will pay $2 million to recover 4 million switches over the next three years. Although current law bans the selling mercury containing switch automobiles in California, there are still many cars on our roadways containing this liability to our public health. Adjust Settings: - In the Shrinkwrap settings, choose "Project" or "Nearest Surface Point" for the mode. - You can adjust other parameters like Offset to fine-tune how closely it fits your mesh. 4. Apply Modifier: - Once satisfied with the fit, you may apply the modifier if needed (though often keeping modifiers non-destructive is a good practice). 5. Convert NURBS Surface to Mesh: - If necessary, convert the NURBS surface back into a mesh for further editing by selecting it and using *Object > Convert To > Mesh from Curve/Meta/Surf/Text*.
